SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper's main claims (improved likelihood and faster training) are supported by experiments and ablations on CIFAR-10 (Sections 3, 3.4) and the discretized logistic mixture likelihood is given a correct, tractable formulation (Equations 1–2). Mathematical content is simple and appears correct, and the authors release code to reproduce results. Minor concerns remain: comparisons are largely empirical on a single dataset without reported variance or statistical tests, and some claims (e.g. downsampling matching dilated convolutions when combined with shortcuts) are argued qualitatively rather than rigorously.

ORIGINALITY.justification: Most proposed modifications are sensible combinations and engineering improvements of existing ideas (discretized continuous mixtures, conditioning on whole pixels, down/up-sampling with U-net style shortcuts, dropout), and the paper cites related prior work for each. The contribution is a pragmatic, well-engineered PixelCNN++ design that meaningfully improves performance, but it is an incremental assembly of known techniques rather than a single, highly novel method.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is organized in standard sections (Introduction, Modifications, Experiments, Ablations, Conclusion) and states the set of modifications clearly (end of paper and Section 2). Equations and ablation descriptions are informative, but the provided text contains repeated paragraphs and some missing numeric/detail contexts (e.g. full tables and figure references), which makes parts harder to follow; overall exposition is adequate but not flawless.
